headline: Woman's 'Emotional Support Cactus' Has Become The Centerpiece Of Family Therapy
summary: The cactus has been named 'Steve.' The therapist is concerned about the attachment. 'He doesn't judge me,' the woman said.
fullBody: Amanda brings her emotional support cactus, Steve, to family therapy. 'He keeps me grounded,' Amanda said. 'Literally, he's a cactus.' The therapist has asked about the relationship. 'It's complicated,' Amanda said. 'He's not very emotionally available. But he's there.' The cactus has been given a chair in the therapy session. 'I think Steve needs his own therapist,' the therapist said. Steve has not responded.
